rationale: Educ. Code §45.106 governs use of county available school fund apportionment for career and technology education. The homestead exemption increase affects local property tax revenue but does not alter county fund apportionment usage rules.
quote: A school district or accumulation of districts that operates a school designated as an area school for career and technology education purposes or that participates in a designated area career and technology education program shall use its annual county available school fund apportionment, if any, in the operation of the area school or program or in financing facilities for the school, notwithstanding any laws to the contrary.
_note: No direct statutory conflict. §45.106 addresses allocation of county available school funds, not homestead exemption levels. Indirect fiscal pressure on school districts is possible but not a textual conflict.
